km.England: ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Aelfric Cild Ealdorman of Merica出生在英國。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於0985年。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Aelfric Cild Ealdorman of Merica出生在英国。他至少有1个儿子。他去世于0985年。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Merica
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of an unidentified German surname, most likely Merck or its variants Merk and Merke.
Romanian: from a pet form of the personal name   Mera  .

History: In North America, the surname Merica first appeared in early 1800s in VA. It is borne by the descendants of a certain Johannes Markey and his (half)brother(s), said to come from Germany. The original form of his surname is not known, as Markey was an altered form, too.

variants.name: Merck
meanings: German and French (mainly Alsace and Lorraine): from a short form of an ancient Germanic compound personal name with initial element mark ‘boundary, border area’, for example, Markwardt .

nameMeanings.name: Cild
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English wilde ‘wild, violent’ (Old English wilde), hence a nickname for a man of violent and undisciplined character, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a patch of overgrown uncultivated land (from Middle English wilde (noun) ‘wild place, wasteland’).
English: variant of Wile , with excrescent -d.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): cognate of 1 above, from Middle High German wilde, wilt, German wild ‘wild’, also used in the sense ‘strange, foreign’, and therefore in some cases a nickname for an incomer.

variants.name: Wilde
meanings: Short form of Mathilde; see Matilda .

variants.name: Wildt
meanings: German: variant of   Wild  .

variants.name: Wile
meanings: Short form of William , in use since the early Middle Ages, when it was occasionally used also for various other given names of Germanic origin containing the first element wil ‘will, desire’ (e.g. Wilbert and Wilmer ). It is now also used as an independent given name.

variants.name: Wilds
meanings: English: variant of Wild , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Wilt
meanings: Short form of William , in use since the early Middle Ages, when it was occasionally used also for various other given names of Germanic origin containing the first element wil ‘will, desire’ (e.g. Wilbert and Wilmer ). It is now also used as an independent given name.

variants.name: Wilderman
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Wild  .
Americanized form of German Wildermann: elaborated form of Wild , with the addition of Middle High German man ‘man’.

variants.name: Wilder
meanings: From an Old English personal name, derived from wil ‘will, desire’ + mǣr ‘famous’. This died out in the Middle Ages, but gave rise to a surname before it did so. The modern given name is probably a transferred use of that surname, perhaps adopted in particular as a masculine form of Wilma .

variants.name: Wylde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Caitlin.
English: variant of   Wild  .

variants.name: Wildermuth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Heinz.
German: variant of Wild , literally ‘wild spirit’, from Middle High German wilder, inflected form of wild + muot sense, attitude, intent, courage'.

historicEvents.title: Kingdom of England is Formed
historicEvents.description: Athelstan, king of Wessex and Mercia, formed the Kingdom of England.

historicEvents.title: Vikings Invade England
historicEvents.description: Viking raiders from Scandinavia invaded England in 980. Years later in 991, the English government paid them 10,000 in hopes for peace. The Viking raiders, however, continued to force more money from England.

en.lifeSummary: Aelfric Cild Ealdorman of Merica was born in England. He had at least 1 son. He died in 0985.
